pattern: Tracking each value's set of live indices (rather than a single index) lets one array-with-swap-pop structure support duplicate values while keeping insert, remove, and uniform getRandom at O(1).
hints: A plain value-to-index map breaks the instant a value can appear twice — you need to know *which* copy you removed.
Map each value to the set of positions it currently occupies in a backing array; insert appends and records the new position.

To remove one copy, pick any recorded position for that value, overwrite it with the array's last element (fixing that moved element's position set), then pop — getRandom is a uniform index into the array.

edge_inputs.description: insert a duplicate, then remove one copy leaving one behind
second insert of the same value must report false even though the collection grows
remove every copy of a value down to zero, then it must be absent from getRandom and re-insertable as new
getRandom over a collection holding a single copy of a single value is fully determined
remove on a value never inserted must return false without touching state
two values with mixed multiplicities, random draws confined to survivors
extreme int values with a duplicate at one extreme

stress.description: random op mix over a small value pool (forces duplicates), getRandom only when non-empty
